---
title: "Medesta paper : dental calculus section"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-02"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is the R Markdown for the Medesta Dental Calculus part
```{r}
## Medesta scripts for dental calculus analysis


#Sourcetracker

library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)
library(compositions)
library(zCompositions)
library(conflicted)

## Make dplyr win conflicts (so select() etc. behave)
conflict_prefer("select", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("filter", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("mutate", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("arrange", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("summarise", "dplyr")
conflicts_prefer(compositions::dist)


library(readxl)

counts <- read_excel("~/Desktop/PhD/Medesta_Calculus/NOV2025/final_cleaned.xlsx")

counts_mat <- counts %>%
  mutate(across(-taxon, ~ as.numeric(.))) %>%
  column_to_rownames("taxon") %>%
  as.matrix()

# Convert to relative abundances per sample
sample_totals <- colSums(counts_mat)
rel_mat <- sweep(counts_mat, 2, sample_totals, "/")

# Filtering thresholds
threshold <- 0.0001      # mean relative abundance cutoff
min_prev <- 2            # must appear in >= 2 samples

# 1. Mean abundance filter
keep_abund <- rowMeans(rel_mat) >= threshold

# 2. Prevalence filter (at least 1 read in >= 2 samples)
keep_prev <- rowSums(counts_mat > 0) >= min_prev

# Combine
keep <- keep_abund & keep_prev

# Filter the table
counts_filtered <- counts_mat[keep, ]

write.table(
  counts_filtered,
  file = "counts_filtered.tsv",
  sep = "\t",
  quote = FALSE,
  row.names = FALSE
)


# This script is to see how total reads per sample and number of species 
# relate in each sample

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# counts_filtered: first column = taxon, others = samples
counts_mat <- as.matrix(counts_filtered[, -1])

sample_names <- colnames(counts_filtered)[-1]

sample_summary <- data.frame(
  Sample      = sample_names,
  Total_reads = colSums(counts_mat),
  Richness    = colSums(counts_mat > 0)   # number of taxa with count > 0
)


richness<-ggplot(sample_summary, aes(x = Total_reads, y = Richness)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Sample), vjust = -0.5, size = 3, check_overlap = TRUE) +
  labs(
    x = "Total reads per sample",
    y = "Number of species (richness)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()  +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, vjust = 0, hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  theme(plot.margin = unit(c(1,1,1,1), "cm"))

ggsave(richness,device = "png",dpi = 300,units = "mm", height = 200, width = 200, filename = "Plot_richnessvsreads.png")


## Clean start

library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)
library(compositions)
library(zCompositions)
library(conflicted)

## Make dplyr win conflicts (so select() etc. behave)
conflict_prefer("select", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("filter", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("mutate", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("arrange", "dplyr")
conflict_prefer("summarise", "dplyr")
conflicts_prefer(compositions::dist)

## Your colors (unchanged)
my_cols <- c(
  "#EE8866", "#77AADD", "#FFAABB", "#99DDFF",
  "#EEDD88", "#44BB99", "#BBCC33", "#AAAA00", "grey"
)

outliers <- c("LOH001A", "TPM007")

meta <- read.delim("NOV2025/metadata_Med.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", check.names = FALSE)

stopifnot("SampleID" %in% colnames(meta))
rownames(meta) <- meta$SampleID

counts <- read_excel("~/Desktop/PhD/Medesta_Calculus/NOV2025/final_cleaned.xlsx")

counts_mat <- counts %>%
  mutate(across(-taxon, ~ as.numeric(.))) %>%
  column_to_rownames("taxon") %>%
  as.matrix()


## keep only samples that are also in metadata
common_samples <- intersect(colnames(counts_mat), rownames(meta))
counts_mat <- counts_mat[, common_samples, drop = FALSE]
meta <- meta[common_samples, , drop = FALSE]

## sanity checks
stopifnot(all(colnames(counts_mat) == rownames(meta)))

## Relative abundances (taxa x samples)
rel_mat <- sweep(counts_mat, 2, colSums(counts_mat), "/")

threshold <- 0.0001  # mean relative abundance cutoff
min_prev  <- 2       # present in >= 2 samples

keep_abund <- rowMeans(rel_mat, na.rm = TRUE) >= threshold
keep_prev  <- rowSums(counts_mat > 0) >= min_prev
keep <- keep_abund & keep_prev

counts_filt_all <- counts_mat[keep, , drop = FALSE]

cat("All-samples filtered taxa:", nrow(counts_filt_all), "\n")


##clr

## CLR requires samples x taxa
X_all <- t(counts_filt_all)

## zero replacement (CZM)
X_all_rep <- cmultRepl(X_all, method = "CZM", output = "p-counts")

## CLR
clr_all <- compositions::clr(X_all_rep)

## Aitchison distance = euclidean on CLR
dist_all <- dist(clr_all, method = "euclidean")



pcoa_all <- cmdscale(dist_all, k = 3, eig = TRUE)
eig_all <- pcoa_all$eig
var_expl_all <- eig_all / sum(eig_all[eig_all > 0])

pcoa_all_df <- data.frame(
  SampleID = rownames(pcoa_all$points),
  PC1 = pcoa_all$points[, 1],
  PC2 = pcoa_all$points[, 2],
  PC3 = pcoa_all$points[, 3]
) %>%
  left_join(meta, by = "SampleID")

p_all_12 <- ggplot(pcoa_all_df, aes(PC1, PC2, color = Site, shape = Origin)) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_cols) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "ALL samples",
    x = sprintf("PCoA1 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_expl_all[1]),
    y = sprintf("PCoA2 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_expl_all[2])
  )

p_all_13 <- ggplot(pcoa_all_df, aes(PC1, PC3, color = Site, shape = Origin)) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_cols) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "ALL samples",
    x = sprintf("PCoA1 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_expl_all[1]),
    y = sprintf("PCoA3 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_expl_all[3])
  )

print(p_all_12)
print(p_all_13)

##Clustering

library(factoextra)

set.seed(123)
gap_plot <- fviz_nbclust(as.matrix(clr_all), kmeans, method = "gap_stat", nboot = 500) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Gap statistic for k-means on CLR (ALL samples)")
print(gap_plot)


k_best <- 2   # 

set.seed(123)
km_all <- kmeans(as.matrix(clr_all), centers = k_best, nstart = 100)
pcoa_all_df$Cluster <- factor(km_all$cluster)

ggplot(pcoa_all_df, aes(PC1, PC2, color = Cluster, shape = Origin)) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = paste0("ALL samples: k-means on CLR (k=", k_best, ")"),
    x = sprintf("PCoA1 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_expl_all[1]),
    y = sprintf("PCoA2 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_expl_all[2])
  )
table(pcoa_all_df$Cluster, pcoa_all_df$Origin)
table(pcoa_all_df$Cluster, pcoa_all_df$County)

##RF

library(randomForest)

rf_df <- pcoa_all_df %>%
  dplyr::select(SampleID, Cluster, Origin, County, Site, Context,
                Lab_batch, Percent_Bacteria_Classified, Avg_GC, Avg_length) %>%
  mutate(
    Cluster = factor(Cluster),
    Origin  = factor(Origin),
    County  = factor(County),
    Site    = factor(Site),
    Context = factor(Context),
    Lab_batch = factor(Lab_batch),
    Percent_Bacteria_Classified = as.numeric(Percent_Bacteria_Classified),
    Avg_GC = as.numeric(Avg_GC),
    Avg_length = as.numeric(Avg_length)
  )

set.seed(123)
rf_fit <- randomForest(
  Cluster ~ Origin + County + Site + Context + Lab_batch +
    Percent_Bacteria_Classified + Avg_GC + Avg_length,
  data = rf_df,
  importance = TRUE,
  ntree = 5000
)

rf_fit
varImpPlot(rf_fit, main = "Random forest: predictors of microbiome clusters")

##permanova

vars_all <- c(
  "Percent_Bacteria_Classified",
  "County",
  "Origin",
  "Site",
  "Avg_GC",
  "Avg_length",
  "Lab_batch"
)

## Match metadata to samples in the distance matrix
sample_ids_all <- attr(dist_all, "Labels")

meta_all <- meta[sample_ids_all, vars_all, drop = FALSE] %>%
  mutate(
    County = factor(County),
    Origin = factor(Origin),
    Site = factor(Site),
    Lab_batch = factor(Lab_batch),
    Percent_Bacteria_Classified =
      as.numeric(Percent_Bacteria_Classified),
    Avg_GC = as.numeric(Avg_GC),
    Avg_length = as.numeric(Avg_length)
  ) %>%
  drop_na()

perm_all <- adonis2(
  dist_all ~ Percent_Bacteria_Classified + County + Avg_GC + Avg_length + Lab_batch,
  data = meta_all,
  permutations = 9999,
  by = "margin"
)
perm_all

## dispersion checks (ONLY for categorical vars)
permutest(betadisper(dist_all, meta_all$Origin), permutations = 9999)
permutest(betadisper(dist_all, meta_all$Site), permutations = 9999)
permutest(betadisper(dist_all, meta_all$Lab_batch), permutations = 9999)


## Make sure it is a factor and there are no NAs in used columns
meta_ap <- meta %>%
  dplyr::select(Percent_Bacteria_Classified, Site, County, Origin, Avg_GC, Avg_length, Lab_batch, Anterior_Posterior) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Origin = factor(Origin),
    Lab_batch = factor(Lab_batch),
    Anterior_Posterior = factor(Anterior_Posterior),
    Percent_Bacteria_Classified = as.numeric(Percent_Bacteria_Classified),
    Avg_GC = as.numeric(Avg_GC),
    Avg_length = as.numeric(Avg_length)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na()

## Subset distance matrix to the same samples (important!)
s_ap <- rownames(meta_ap)
dist_ap <- as.dist(as.matrix(dist_all)[s_ap, s_ap])

## PERMANOVA (marginal effects)
perm_ap <- adonis2(
  dist_ap ~ Percent_Bacteria_Classified + Origin + Avg_GC + Avg_length + Lab_batch + Anterior_Posterior,
  data = meta_ap,
  permutations = 9999,
  by = "margin"
)
perm_ap

## Dispersion check for Anterior_Posterior
permutest(betadisper(dist_ap, meta_ap$Anterior_Posterior), permutations = 9999)



meta_tt <- meta %>%
  dplyr::select(Percent_Bacteria_Classified, Site, County, Origin, Avg_GC, Avg_length, Lab_batch, Tooth_Type) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Origin = factor(Origin),
    Lab_batch = factor(Lab_batch),
    Tooth_Type = factor(Tooth_Type),
    Percent_Bacteria_Classified = as.numeric(Percent_Bacteria_Classified),
    Avg_GC = as.numeric(Avg_GC),
    Avg_length = as.numeric(Avg_length)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na()

s_tt <- rownames(meta_tt)
dist_tt <- as.dist(as.matrix(dist_all)[s_tt, s_tt])

perm_tt <- adonis2(
  dist_tt ~ Percent_Bacteria_Classified + Origin + Avg_GC + Avg_length + Lab_batch + Tooth_Type,
  data = meta_tt,
  permutations = 9999,
  by = "margin"
)
perm_tt


perm_site <- adonis2(
  dist_tt ~ Percent_Bacteria_Classified + Site + Avg_GC + Avg_length + Lab_batch + Tooth_Type,
  data = meta_tt,
  permutations = 9999,
  by = "margin"
)
perm_site

## Dispersion check for Tooth_Type
permutest(betadisper(dist_tt, meta_tt$Tooth_Type), permutations = 9999)

####NOW everything without outliers

## Remove outlier columns at the beginning
keep_samples_no <- setdiff(colnames(counts_mat), outliers)

counts_mat_no <- counts_mat[, keep_samples_no, drop = FALSE]
meta_no <- meta[keep_samples_no, , drop = FALSE]

cat("Samples ALL:", ncol(counts_mat), "\n")
cat("Samples NO outliers:", ncol(counts_mat_no), "\n")

## Recompute rel abund and filtering on the new table
rel_mat_no <- sweep(counts_mat_no, 2, colSums(counts_mat_no), "/")

keep_abund_no <- rowMeans(rel_mat_no, na.rm = TRUE) >= threshold
keep_prev_no  <- rowSums(counts_mat_no > 0) >= min_prev
keep_no <- keep_abund_no & keep_prev_no

counts_filt_no <- counts_mat_no[keep_no, , drop = FALSE]
cat("No-outliers filtered taxa:", nrow(counts_filt_no), "\n")


X_no <- t(counts_filt_no)
X_no_rep <- cmultRepl(X_no, method = "CZM", output = "p-counts")
clr_no <- compositions::clr(X_no_rep)
dist_no <- dist(clr_no, method = "euclidean")

pcoa_no <- cmdscale(dist_no, k = 3, eig = TRUE)
eig_no <- pcoa_no$eig
var_expl_no <- eig_no / sum(eig_no[eig_no > 0])

pcoa_no_df <- data.frame(
  SampleID = rownames(pcoa_no$points),
  PC1 = pcoa_no$points[, 1],
  PC2 = pcoa_no$points[, 2],
  PC3 = pcoa_no$points[, 3]
) %>%
  left_join(meta_no, by = "SampleID")

p_no_12 <- ggplot(pcoa_no_df, aes(PC1, PC2, color = Site, shape = Origin)) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_cols) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = sprintf("PCoA1 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_expl_no[1]),
    y = sprintf("PCoA2 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_expl_no[2])
  )

p_no_13 <- ggplot(pcoa_no_df, aes(PC1, PC3, color = Site, shape = Origin)) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_cols) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = sprintf("PCoA1 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_expl_no[1]),
    y = sprintf("PCoA3 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_expl_no[3])
  )

print(p_no_12)
print(p_no_13)


library(factoextra)

set.seed(123)
gap_plot <- fviz_nbclust(as.matrix(clr_no), kmeans, method = "gap_stat", nboot = 500) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Gap statistic for k-means on CLR (ALL samples)")
print(gap_plot)


##if we take out outliers there is no such thing as clusters because the cluster indicated in 1

meta_no2 <- pcoa_no_df %>%
  dplyr::select(SampleID, Origin, Site, County, Lab_batch,
                Percent_Bacteria_Classified, Avg_GC, Avg_length, Anterior_Posterior, Tooth_Type) %>%
  mutate(
    Origin = factor(Origin),
    Site = factor(Site),
    County = factor(County),
    Lab_batch = factor(Lab_batch),
    Anterior_Posterior = factor(Anterior_Posterior),
    Tooth_Type = factor(Tooth_Type)
  )

perm_no <- adonis2(
  dist_no ~ Percent_Bacteria_Classified + Origin + Avg_GC + Avg_length + Lab_batch + Anterior_Posterior,
  data = meta_no2,
  permutations = 9999,
  by = "margin"
)
perm_no

perm_no2 <- adonis2(
  dist_no ~ Percent_Bacteria_Classified + Tooth_Type + Origin + Avg_GC + Avg_length + Lab_batch,
  data = meta_no2,
  permutations = 9999,
  by = "margin"
)
perm_no2

perm_no3 <- adonis2(
  dist_no ~ Percent_Bacteria_Classified + Tooth_Type + County + Avg_GC + Avg_length + Lab_batch,
  data = meta_no2,
  permutations = 9999,
  by = "margin"
)
perm_no3

permutest(betadisper(dist_no, meta_no2$Origin), permutations = 9999)
permutest(betadisper(dist_no, meta_no2$Site), permutations = 9999)
permutest(betadisper(dist_no, meta_no2$Lab_batch), permutations = 9999)


# install.packages("gld")
# 
# BiocManager::install("ANCOMBC", force=TRUE)


###############################################################################
## Oral microbiome – ordination drivers + differential abundance
## Strategy:
##  - PCoA (CLR / Aitchison) = community structure
##  - envfit = taxa associated with ordination axes
###############################################################################

## -------------------------------
## 0. Libraries
## -------------------------------

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)
library(compositions)
library(phyloseq)
library(ANCOMBC)

## -------------------------------
## 1. User-defined parameters
## -------------------------------

outliers <- c("LOH001A", "TPM007")

my_cols <- c(
  "#EE8866", "#77AADD", "#FFAABB", "#99DDFF",
  "#EEDD88", "#44BB99", "#BBCC33", "#AAAA00", "grey"
)

N_envfit <- 20   # number of taxa to highlight

## -------------------------------
## 2. INPUT OBJECTS 
## -------------------------------
## From previous pipeline:
##   counts_filt_all : taxa × samples count matrix (ALL samples)
##   counts_filt_no  : taxa × samples count matrix (NO outliers)
##   clr_all         : samples × taxa CLR matrix (ALL)
##   clr_no          : samples × taxa CLR matrix (NO outliers)
##   pcoa_all        : cmdscale object (ALL)
##   pcoa_no         : cmdscale object (NO outliers)
##   pcoa_all_df     : PCoA scores + metadata (ALL)
##   pcoa_no_df      : PCoA scores + metadata (NO outliers)
##   meta            : full metadata table (SampleID column present)

## -------------------------------
## 3. envfit on PCoA – ALL samples
## -------------------------------

## Site scores (PC1 / PC2)
site_all <- as.data.frame(pcoa_all$points[, 1:2])
colnames(site_all) <- c("PC1", "PC2")

## Taxa matrix for envfit (CLR values)
tax_all <- as.data.frame(clr_all)
tax_all <- tax_all[rownames(site_all), , drop = FALSE]

## Run envfit
set.seed(1)
fit_all <- envfit(site_all, tax_all, permutations = 9999)

## Extract taxa vectors
envfit_all <- as.data.frame(fit_all$vectors$arrows)
envfit_all$Taxon <- rownames(envfit_all)
envfit_all$r2 <- fit_all$vectors$r
envfit_all$p  <- fit_all$vectors$pvals

## Significant taxa aligned with ordination
envfit_sig_all <- envfit_all %>%
  arrange(p) %>%
  filter(p < 0.05)

## Select top N taxa for downstream interpretation
taxa_focus <- envfit_sig_all %>%
  slice_head(n = N_envfit) %>%
  pull(Taxon)

taxa_focus

## -------------------------------
## 4. Relative abundances – ALL samples
## -------------------------------

rel_all <- sweep(counts_filt_all, 2, colSums(counts_filt_all), "/")

meta_all_groups <- pcoa_all_df %>%
  transmute(
    SampleID,
    Origin = as.character(Origin),
    Origin2 = if_else(Origin == "Rural", "Rural", "Urban"),
    OutlierStatus = if_else(SampleID %in% outliers, "Outlier", "Other")
  )

rel_all_long <- rel_all[taxa_focus, , drop = FALSE] %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("Taxon") %>%
  pivot_longer(-Taxon, names_to = "SampleID", values_to = "RelAbund") %>%
  left_join(meta_all_groups, by = "SampleID")

## Plot: ALL samples (explicit outliers)
ggplot(rel_all_long, aes(x = OutlierStatus, y = RelAbund)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ Taxon, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "ALL samples: taxa associated with ordination (envfit)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Relative abundance"
  )

## -------------------------------
## 5. Relative abundances – NO outliers
## -------------------------------

rel_no <- sweep(counts_filt_no, 2, colSums(counts_filt_no), "/")

meta_no_groups <- pcoa_no_df %>%
  transmute(
    SampleID,
    Origin = as.character(Origin),
    Origin2 = if_else(Origin == "Rural", "Rural", "Urban")
  )

rel_no_long <- rel_no[taxa_focus, , drop = FALSE] %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("Taxon") %>%
  pivot_longer(-Taxon, names_to = "SampleID", values_to = "RelAbund") %>%
  left_join(meta_no_groups, by = "SampleID")

## Plot: NO outliers (Urban vs Rural)
ggplot(rel_no_long, aes(x = Origin2, y = RelAbund)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ Taxon, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "NO outliers: taxa associated with ordination (envfit)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Relative abundance"
  )


###############################################################################
## OUTLIER COMPOSITION SCRIPT
##  - relative abundance profiles
##  - top taxa and percentages
##  - top-20 overlap
##  - log2 fold-change (outliers vs all others) on mean relative abundance
###############################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(readr)

## -------------------------------
## 1) Inputs you must have
## -------------------------------
## counts_filt_all: taxa x samples count matrix (numeric)
## rownames = taxa names, colnames = SampleIDs

# If your matrix is named differently, set it here:
otu <- counts_filt_all

## define outliers
outliers <- c("LOH001A", "TPM007")

stopifnot(all(outliers %in% colnames(otu)))

## -------------------------------
## 2) Relative abundances
## -------------------------------

rel <- sweep(otu, 2, colSums(otu), "/")   # taxa x samples, each sample sums to 1

## -------------------------------
## 3) Top taxa in each outlier sample (with percentages)
## -------------------------------

get_top <- function(rel_vec, n = 20) {
  x <- sort(rel_vec, decreasing = TRUE)
  x <- head(x, n)
  data.frame(
    Taxon = names(x),
    RelAbund = as.numeric(x),
    Percent = 100 * as.numeric(x)
  )
}

top_LOH <- get_top(rel[, "LOH001A"], n = 20) %>% mutate(SampleID = "LOH001A")
top_TPM <- get_top(rel[, "TPM007"],  n = 20) %>% mutate(SampleID = "TPM007")

## write tables
write_tsv(top_LOH, "outlier_LOH001A_top20_relative_abundance.tsv")
write_tsv(top_TPM, "outlier_TPM007_top20_relative_abundance.tsv")

## -------------------------------
## 4) Overlap of top-20 taxa
## -------------------------------

shared_top20 <- intersect(top_LOH$Taxon, top_TPM$Taxon)
n_shared <- length(shared_top20)

cat("\nShared taxa in top 20:", n_shared, "out of 20\n")
print(shared_top20)

write_tsv(data.frame(Taxon = shared_top20),
          "outliers_shared_top20_taxa.tsv")

## -------------------------------
## 5) Quick “paragraph-ready” dominance summary (top 3 taxa)
## -------------------------------

dominant_LOH <- top_LOH %>% slice(1:6) %>% select(Taxon, Percent)
dominant_TPM <- top_TPM %>% slice(1:6) %>% select(Taxon, Percent)

cat("\nLOH001A top taxa:\n")
print(dominant_LOH)

cat("\nTPM007 top taxa:\n")
print(dominant_TPM)

## -------------------------------
## 6) Log2 fold-change (outliers vs all others)
##    computed on MEAN relative abundance per group
## -------------------------------

group <- ifelse(colnames(rel) %in% outliers, "Outlier", "Other")

mean_outlier <- rowMeans(rel[, group == "Outlier", drop = FALSE])
mean_other   <- rowMeans(rel[, group == "Other", drop = FALSE])

## pseudocount to avoid log(0); 1e-6 is fine for relative abundances
log2fc <- log2((mean_outlier + 1e-6) / (mean_other + 1e-6))

fc_table <- data.frame(
  Taxon = rownames(rel),
  mean_outlier = mean_outlier,
  mean_other = mean_other,
  log2FC = log2fc
) %>%
  arrange(desc(log2FC))

## top enriched taxa in outliers
top_enriched <- fc_table %>% slice_head(n = 20)
write_tsv(fc_table, "outliers_vs_others_log2FC_all_taxa.tsv")
write_tsv(top_enriched, "outliers_vs_others_log2FC_top30.tsv")

cat("\nTop taxa enriched in outliers (log2FC):\n")
print(top_enriched)

## -------------------------------
## 7) OPTIONAL: export a single compact table for the paper paragraph
##    (top 20 in each outlier + log2FC)
## -------------------------------

paper_tab <- bind_rows(top_LOH, top_TPM) %>%
  left_join(fc_table %>% select(Taxon, log2FC), by = "Taxon") %>%
  arrange(SampleID, desc(RelAbund))

write_tsv(paper_tab, "outliers_paper_ready_table_top20_plus_log2FC.tsv")

cat("\nSaved outputs:\n",
    "- outlier_LOH001A_top20_relative_abundance.tsv\n",
    "- outlier_TPM007_top20_relative_abundance.tsv\n",
    "- outliers_shared_top20_taxa.tsv\n",
    "- outliers_vs_others_log2FC_top30.tsv\n",
    "- outliers_paper_ready_table_top20_plus_log2FC.tsv\n")


###############################################################################
## ANCOMBC Script
##  - phyloseq object for better handling
##  - run ancombc
##  - plot results Urban commoners vs Rural
##  - plot results Urban elite vs Rural 
###############################################################################

library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(ANCOMBC)

# counts_filt_no: taxa x samples
# meta_no: metadata with SampleID column

# 1) Keep only shared samples, AND force same order
common <- intersect(colnames(counts_filt_no), meta_no$SampleID)

counts_filt_no <- counts_filt_no[, common, drop = FALSE]
meta_no <- meta_no %>% filter(SampleID %in% common)


# IMPORTANT: reorder metadata to match OTU table columns
meta_no <- meta_no %>% slice(match(common, SampleID))

# 2) Make sample_data with SampleID as rownames
meta_no_ps <- meta_no
rownames(meta_no_ps) <- meta_no_ps$SampleID
meta_no_ps$SampleID <- NULL   # remove column so it doesn't duplicate info

# 3) Sanity check: must be identical
stopifnot(all(colnames(counts_filt_no) == rownames(meta_no_ps)))

# 4) Build phyloseq
OTU <- otu_table(as.matrix(counts_filt_no), taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
SAM <- sample_data(meta_no_ps)

ps_no <- phyloseq(OTU, SAM)
ps_no

set.seed(1)

out_no <- ancombc2(
  data = ps_no,
  fix_formula = "Origin + Percent_Bacteria_Classified + Anterior_Posterior + Avg_GC",
  group = "Origin",
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.05, #the taxon has to be present in at least 5% of the samples to be included
  lib_cut = 0, #this is to exclude samples with low library count, but we already did this filtering so here i didn't add cut-offs
  s0_perc = 0.05,
  struc_zero = TRUE, #detects taxa truly absent from samples
  neg_lb = TRUE,
  pseudo_sens = TRUE, #runs it with different pseudocounts
  global = TRUE, #tests overall diversity
  pairwise = TRUE, #tests pairwise comparisons
  dunnet = FALSE, #false because not applicable to my data
  trend = FALSE, #false because not applicable to my data
  n_cl = 1,
  verbose = TRUE 
)

res_global <- out_no$res_global
res_global
res_pair   <- out_no$res_pair
res_pair
zero_tab   <- out_no$zero_ind

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Comparison Rural - Urban elite
df_plot1 <- res_pair %>%
  transmute(
    Taxon = taxon,
    logFC = lfc_OriginUrban_elite,     
    qval  = q_OriginUrban_elite,
    robust = passed_ss_OriginUrban_elite
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(logFC)) %>%
  arrange(logFC)

df_plot1 <- df_plot1 %>%
  mutate(
    sig = case_when(
      qval < 0.001 & robust ~ "***",
      qval < 0.01  & robust ~ "**",
      qval < 0.05  & robust ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )
  )

df_plot_top1 <- bind_rows(
  head(df_plot1, 20),   # most negative
  tail(df_plot1, 20)    # most positive
)

ggplot(df_plot_top1,
       aes(x = logFC,
           y = reorder(Taxon, logFC))) +
         geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sig),
    hjust = ifelse(df_plot_top1$logFC > 0, -0.2, 1.2),
    size = 4
    ) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Log fold change",
    y = "Species",
    title = "Rural vs Urban elite (ANCOM-BC2)"
  ) +
  
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )

# Comparison Rural - Urban commoners
df_plot <- res_pair %>%
  transmute(
    Taxon = taxon,
    logFC = lfc_OriginUrban_commoners,     # adjust name if needed
    qval  = q_OriginUrban_commoners,
    robust = passed_ss_OriginUrban_commoners
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(logFC)) %>%
  arrange(logFC)

df_plot <- df_plot %>%
  mutate(
    sig = case_when(
      qval < 0.001 & robust ~ "***",
      qval < 0.01  & robust ~ "**",
      qval < 0.05  & robust ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )
  )

df_plot_top <- bind_rows(
  head(df_plot, 20),   # most negative
  tail(df_plot, 20)    # most positive
)

ggplot(df_plot_top,
       aes(x = logFC,
           y = reorder(Taxon, logFC))) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sig),
    hjust = ifelse(df_plot_top$logFC > 0, -0.2, 1.2),
    size = 4
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Log fold change",
    y = "Species",
    title = "Rural vs Urban commoners (ANCOM-BC2)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())

```
